channel.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: dor
item.description:
Right now I see that after I ran ‘npx frontity build’ the code splitting works well. I don’t sure why but at least it works well…

TNX

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: dor
item.description:
Hi Juanma, thanks for your response.

This is the link to the repo.

(You should clone the repo, and then run the project in your local environment with the ‘npx run dev’ command).

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Hi @dor,

Can you please provide a repo or code-sandbox with your code? This is especially helpful to find solutions to technical issues with specific code

Detailing the info suggested here when having issues will help the community to provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible.

Your Github repository should work directly in codeSandbox using this link: https://codesandbox.io/s/github/Sani-admin/test

Can you specify the errors you’re getting and prepare the repository to throw just the error reported in this thread?

In the meantime, here you have the documentation for Code Splitting with Frontity

docs.frontity.org

Code Splitting

In this case Frontity is just centralizing the use of the Loadable Components library

loadable-components.com

Getting Started - Loadable Components

The recommended Code Splitting library for React.

so have a look at their docs to learn more about how to use this library in specific use cases

It seems this issue has already been reported in their repository:

  • "cannot find main in stats" · Issue #178 · gregberge/loadable-components · GitHub
  • "loadable: cannot find x in stats" with dynamic import and loadable.lib · Issue #382 · gregberge/loadable-components · GitHub

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: Johan
item.description:
I did some research, and checked out your repo.

As far as I can see you do everything correct, however I’m not an expert
And I tried to run it locally, but obviously failed without access to your (local) api.

However I did notice the same error on Google, which is related to the chunkNames created by Webpack. As far as I could understand it the problem was caused by having additional - in file or directory names which can mess up Webpack (although I didn’t notice any direct problems when building your project).

In my own project I have something like this, which is basically identical to what you have, and it works without any problems.

// theme/index.js

import { connect, Head, loadable } from "frontity";
import Switch from "@frontity/components/switch";

const Loading = loadable(() => import('./loading'));
const PageError = loadable(() => import('./page-error'));
// more components, both direct and through loadable

const Theme = ({ state }) => {
    // Get information about the current URL.
    const data = state.source.get(state.router.link);

    return (
        <>
            {/* ... */}
            <Switch>
                {/* ... */}
                <Loading when={data.isFetching} />
                <PageError when={data.isError} />
            </Switch>
            {/* ... */}
        </>
    );
};

export default connect(Theme);

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: dor
item.description:
Hi Johan, thanks for your response.

This is the link to the rep

(The last commit is without the route code-splitting).

TNX !

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: codemonkeynorth
item.description:
Tagging this thread as it seems to be a similar issue

How can I implement component-centric code splitting? 🙋Get Help
Using loadable from frontity, I am able to do route-centric code splitting. Works great! Now I want to go a step further, and do component-centric splitting for a given route. But whenever I use loadable for component-centric splitting I get the following error: Invariant Violation: loadable: cannot find topArticle in stats topArticle => component for which I am trying to do code splitting. How can I implement component-centric splitting?

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: Johan
item.description:
It would help to see a bit more code than this snippet, since the problem is most likely in one of the 2 scripts you try to load with loadable(), and based on the message an education guess would be that you have an array or object called stats and use a key x which isn’t set properly.

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: dor
item.description:
codemonkeynorth:

Looks like it should essentially be the same though?

I tried… steel get the same error

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: codemonkeynorth
item.description:
Have you tried doing it the way Mars theme does it?

list.js
export default loadable(() => import("./list"));

Not tried it but maybe worth looking at?

Looks like it should essentially be the same though?

Read full topic

item.title: Lodable cannot find 'x' in stats
item.dc:creator: dor
item.description:
Hi,

I would like to use ‘lodable’ in order to split my routes into various bundles.

But when i’m trying to use it, I get this kind of error:
“Invariant Violation: loadable: cannot find ‘x’ in stats”.

Here is a code snippet of my index.js:

import React from "react";
import Switch from "@frontity/components/switch";
import { connect, loadable } from "frontity";

// main templates
import MainFooter from "../templates/main-templates/main-footer";

// inner templates
import HomePage from "../templates/inner-templates/home-page";

// lodable templates
const About = loadable(() => import("../templates/inner-templates/about"));
const Properties = loadable(() => import("../templates/inner-templates/properties"));

// global style
import GlobalStyle from "../global-style";

const Root = ({ state }) => {
  const data = state.source.get(state.router.link);

  return (
    <>
      <GlobalStyle />
        <Switch>
            <HomePage when={data.isHome} />
            <About when={data.link === "/about/"} />
            <Properties when={data.link === "/properties/"} />
        </Switch>

      <MainFooter />
    </>
  );
};

export default connect(Root);

How can I solve this issue ?

TNX !

Read full topic
